architecture: Runtime Governance, Operational Admissibility, Enterprise TrustMap, Sandeep Practice-Builder Mode, Manual Evidence Intake, CRM-Lite Workflow, Executive Proof Pack, and Architecture Transition Plan
core_thesis: AI is controlled before it acts, or it is not controlled at all.
core_question: Should this action be operationally admissible right now?

release_train.v21: Executive Demo QA and Navigation Compression
release_train.v26: Persistence, Intake, and CRM Payload Hardening
release_train.v30: TrustMap Usability and Executive De-Branding Cleanup
release_train.v31: Decision Engine Depth and Rationale Trace
release_train.v32: Executive Proof Pack and Board Output Upgrade
release_train.v37: Sandeep Demo and Sales Conversion Build

current_capabilities: clean CyberShield blue enterprise SaaS interface
Enterprise TrustMap with center organization node and domain clusters
operational trust score rail and active risk rails
TrustMap edge hover glow and endpoint connector dots
manual evidence intake with local confidence impact
CRM-lite row generator and CSV output
zero-cost prototype to low-cost pilot to enterprise-grade architecture path

strategic_direction.ownership: MJC-owned advisory software, not standalone SaaS yet
strategic_direction.tooling_preference: low-cost and inspectable third-party tools when useful
strategic_direction.visual_style: clean CyberShield blue enterprise SaaS, not full dark command center

crm_direction.sync_status: row payload and CSV output only

critical_ux_rule: Every visible executive object must either explain itself on hover/focus or navigate somewhere useful. No dead bubbles.
critical_architecture_rule: CyberShield must remain consolidated and performant. Do not reintroduce old phase-file module sprawl, broad MutationObserver loops, dynamic script injection, or timestamp-based runtime loading.
prototype_boundary: The current public build is a lightweight static prototype with local/export-oriented behavior only.
commercial_entry_wedge: CyberShield Readiness Review or Operational Trust Assessment led by Maximum Justice Cybersecurity and Dr. Max Justice, vCISO, Security SME, Cybersecurity SME, U.S. veteran, and creator of The CHN vCISO GPT powered by Cyber Shield.
